transcript:
[Meeting date: 2026-08-03]
[00:00] You: Finance quarterly review — Q2 actuals vs. plan.
[00:40] Speaker 1: Q2 revenue came in at $4.2M vs. $4.0M plan — 5% above. Gross margin was 68%, slightly below the 70% target. The shortfall is from higher cloud infrastructure costs.
[01:30] Speaker 2: On the cost side, we overspent on software licenses by $45k. I'll review all SaaS contracts and identify redundant tools by August 10th.
[02:10] Speaker 3: I'll also audit our cloud spend — we've been overprovisioning in several regions. I'll prepare a rightsizing report by August 12th.
[02:50] You: Good. What about the Q3 forecast?
[03:05] Speaker 1: Q3 forecast looks strong — $4.8M. But we need to confirm the enterprise pipeline. Speaker 4, where are we on Pegasus Corp?
[03:30] Speaker 4: Pegasus is at contract stage. If we close by August 15th, it adds $200k to Q3. I'll push for signature this week.
[04:00] Speaker 2: We should also look at our collections process. Three accounts are 60+ days overdue. I'll reach out to each account by August 5th.
[04:35] Speaker 3: I'll update the monthly P&L model to reflect the new cloud cost baseline. I'll have an updated version for the board by August 17th.
[05:00] You: We also need to prepare for the board meeting. Speaker 1, can you own the board package?
[05:15] Speaker 1: I'll prepare the board financial package — Q2 summary, Q3 forecast, updated annual guidance — by August 20th.
[05:45] Speaker 4: I should flag: if we close Pegasus before August 15th, I'll update the Q3 forecast in the board package with the revised number.
[06:05] You: Good. Annual audit prep starts September 1st — let's make sure all Q2 financials are reconciled before then.

I'll prepare a rightsizing report by August 12th.
